name: server
context: Build MCP servers that expose tools, resources, and prompts to AI agents via JSON-RPC over stdio or HTTP
info: An MCP server is a program that exposes capabilities to AI applications through the Model Context Protocol. Servers declare which primitives they support during initialization — tools for executable functions, resources for contextual data, and prompts for interaction templates. The Python SDK provides FastMCP for rapid development using decorators; the TypeScript SDK uses McpServer with Zod schemas. Servers run over stdio (launched as subprocesses by the host) or Streamable HTTP (running as independent HTTP services). Every server must handle the initialization handshake, capability negotiation, and JSON-RPC message routing. Use the MCP Inspector (`npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector`) for interactive testing during development.
triggers: set up a new MCP server
add tools to my MCP server
write an MCP server in python
write an MCP server in typescript
how to build an MCP server

sections.title: Python FastMCP development
sections.body: FastMCP is the high-level Python API for building MCP servers. Import from `mcp.server.fastmcp`, instantiate with a server name, and use `@mcp.tool()`, `@mcp.resource()`, and `@mcp.prompt()` decorators. FastMCP auto-generates JSON Schema from Python type hints and docstrings. Call `mcp.run()` to start — defaults to stdio transport. Requires Python 3.10+ and `mcp` package (`pip install 'mcp[cli]'`). Use `uv` for project management.

sections.title: TypeScript SDK development
sections.body: Use `McpServer` from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js`. Register tools with `server.tool(name, description, schema, handler)` using Zod schemas for input validation. Register resources with `server.resource(name, uri, handler)`. Connect via `StdioServerTransport` or `StreamableHTTPServerTransport`. Install with `npm install @modelcontextprotocol/sdk zod`.

sections.title: Capability negotiation
sections.body: During initialization, the server declares which capabilities it supports in the `capabilities` object: `tools` (with optional `listChanged`), `resources` (with optional `subscribe` and `listChanged`), and `prompts` (with optional `listChanged`). The client reads these capabilities to know which `*/list` and `*/call` methods are available. If a capability is not declared, the client must not attempt to use it.

sections.title: Testing and debugging
sections.body: Use the MCP Inspector (`npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector`) for interactive testing — it provides a web UI to list tools/resources/prompts, call tools, and inspect JSON-RPC messages. For stdio servers, never print to stdout as it corrupts the protocol. Use `stderr` or structured logging. For integration testing, use the MCP client SDK to programmatically connect and verify behavior.
